<title>Logbook — Checkpoint64</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Release notes and write-ups from the Checkpoint64 team." />
  <meta name="robots" content="index, follow" />
  <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark" />
  <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="../retro_save_icon.svg" />
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin />
  <link rel="preload" as="style" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=VT323&family=Press+Start+2P&family=Patrick+Hand&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500;700&display=swap" onload="this.onload=null;this.rel='stylesheet'" />
  <noscript><link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=VT323&family=Press+Start+2P&family=Patrick+Hand&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500;700&display=swap" /></noscript>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="../blog.css" />
  <script>
    (function () {
      try {
        var saved = localStorage.getItem('cp64-theme');
        var theme = saved || (window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: light)').matches ? 'light' : 'dark');
        if (theme === 'light') document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', 'light');
      } catch (e) { /* localStorage blocked */ }
    })();
  </script>

CHECKPOINT64
All posts →

LOGBOOK

Notes from the Checkpoint64 team.

  • 📌 Pinned

    Ditch the dedicated server; your co-op group is probably paying for empty hours

    2026-05-20

    A 24/7 rented box is great when ten people play every night. For the average co-op group, it's $120–$240 a year of idle uptime. Here's the math, and how Checkpoint64 covers ~90% of the use case for a one-time payment.

  • Restore Previous Save State Without the Panic

    2026-07-20

    Restore previous save state after a crash, bad mod or co-op mishap. Keep every version of your PC game saves without folder archaeology after midnight.

  • Game Save Version History Stops Bad Sessions

    2026-07-18

    Game save version history lets you undo corrupted files, bad mod installs and co-op disasters without turning backup folders into a second job for everyone

  • Best Tools for Save Recovery for PC Gamers

    2026-07-16

    Compare the best tools for save recovery, from versioned cloud backups to file recovery apps, and stop one bad session deleting your whole campaign today.

  • Skyrim Modded Save Backup Without the Funeral

    2026-07-14

    Set up a Skyrim modded save backup that survives crashes, bad load orders and accidental overwrites, with version history for every Dragonborn disaster.

  • Project Zomboid Save Recovery Without Starting Over

    2026-07-12

    Project Zomboid save recovery steps for corrupted worlds, bad mod loads and co-op disasters - plus backup habits that stop your next apocalypse cold wipe.

  • RetroArch Save State Backup Without the Panic

    2026-07-11

    RetroArch save state backup explained: protect slots, keep rollback points, and avoid cloud-sync disasters without babysitting folders by hand every week.

  • Elden Ring Save Recovery Without the Panic

    2026-07-10

    Elden Ring save recovery on PC: what still works, what usually fails, and how to restore lost progress without making the mess worse.

  • Valheim Host World Sharing Without the Faff

    2026-07-09

    Valheim host world sharing explained: how to pass a world safely, avoid overwrites, fix host lock-in, and keep co-op progress intact.

  • Factorio World Rollback Without the Panic

    2026-07-08

    Need a factorio world rollback after a bad save, desync, or mod mess? Here's how to restore a base safely and avoid losing hours again.

  • Stardew Valley Co-op Save Share, Sorted

    2026-07-07

    Stardew Valley co-op save share without the usual chaos. Keep every version, hand off host saves safely, and stop losing farms to bad file swaps.

  • Best Minecraft World Backup Tool for PC

    2026-07-06

    Looking for a minecraft world backup tool? Here’s what actually matters for automatic backups, rollbacks, co-op safety and modded save recovery.

  • How to Sync Saves Across PCs Without Drama

    2026-07-05

    Learn how to sync saves across PCs without broken worlds, missing files or dodgy folder hacks. Keep progress current on every machine.

  • Dedicated Server Alternative for Friends

    2026-07-04

    Need a dedicated server alternative for friends? Here’s when save sharing, backups, and host handoff beat paying monthly for a server.

  • How to Share Co-op Worlds Without Drama

    2026-07-03

    Learn how to share co-op worlds without overwrites, missing host files, or dodgy manual backups. Safer handoffs, fewer arguments, more play.

  • Cloud Backup for PC Games That Saves Runs

    2026-07-01

    Cloud backup for PC games stops lost saves, bad rollbacks and co-op chaos. Here's how it works, where it fails, and what to use instead.

  • How to Restore Corrupted Save Files Fast

    2026-06-30

    Learn how to restore corrupted save files fast on PC, recover older versions, find backup folders, and avoid losing your world again for good.

  • Best Game Save Manager for PC Players

    2026-06-29

    Looking for the best game save manager? Here’s what actually matters for PC players, from backups and restores to co-op sharing and mod safety.

  • How to Back Up Game Saves Without the Faff

    2026-06-29

    Learn how to back up game saves properly on PC, avoid corrupted files, recover bad sessions, and keep co-op worlds safe without faff.

  • The Steam launch is going great; what we're building next

    2026-06-27

    A few days in, wishlists are climbing and the playtest is full of strange, wonderful saves. Thank you. A quick note on how it's going and what comes next.

  • Checkpoint64 is on Steam! Wishlist it and join the playtest

    2026-06-24

    There's now a Checkpoint64 page on Steam. You can wishlist it, follow for the launch, and get into the playtest — and yes, the irony of a save-backup tool living next to Steam Cloud is the whole point. Here's what the listing means and what it doesn't change.

  • Back up the whole folder; the save files your game writes later won't slip through

    2026-06-18

    Picking save files by hand has a blind spot — it never notices the ones your game writes after you set it up. A new farm, a fresh profile, an extra slot. The next build can watch the whole save folder and keep catching whatever lands in it. Restoring got safer too. Here's what's new.

  • Anatomy of a save corruption; what actually happens on disk

    2026-06-11

    Corruption sounds like bad luck, but it's usually one of a handful of very specific things going wrong at the byte level — almost always during the write. Here's what a save file really is, the three ways it gets mangled, and why a single save slot is a single point of failure.

  • The free plan just grew a team; try co-op without a card

    2026-06-10

    Free accounts can now create one team — invite your co-op partner, pass the lock, read the logbook, all without paying. Lifetime quietly went from 2 teams to 3 while we were at it. Here's what changed and why.

  • Cloud saves aren't backups; sync will happily overwrite your good save

    2026-06-09

    Steam Cloud, GOG Galaxy, and console cloud saves keep one copy in sync across your machines. That's not the same as a backup. The moment a save corrupts or a bad autosave fires, sync does its job perfectly and pushes the broken file everywhere. Here's the difference, and why versioning is the thing you actually wanted.

  • The perfect companion for emulators; real version history for save states

    2026-06-05

    Emulators already let you save anywhere. What they don't give you is a history. Checkpoint64 watches the save folder your emulator writes to and turns every save into a versioned, restorable timeline — a rewind button that survives across sessions, machines, and emulator updates.

  • Community servers; host a world, share it with a crowd, keep it pristine

    2026-06-01

    A creator can now host a save and hand out a join code that lets an audience download the world without being able to touch it. No write access, no accidental overwrites, capped and revocable. Here's what read-only members are and when you'd want them.

  • Welcome to the Checkpoint64 logbook

    2026-05-19

    First entry. What we're building, why we're building it, and how this blog works.
← Back to checkpoint64.com
